purpose: Check whether the standard-EP top-8 hot-helper path still reproduces an uplift over a plain standard-EP baseline before attributing small MORI-native balanced-MoE gains.

launcher_fix.reason: The active docker path did not forward CANARY_DSV4_NUM_MTP_MODULES / CANARY_DSV4_MTP_LOSS_WEIGHT, while config_registry now defaults TORCHTITAN_DSV4_NUM_MTP_MODULES to 1.
launcher_fix.validation: bash -n passed; retry header printed dsv4_num_mtp_modules=0 and model parameter count dropped from about 87.08B to 80.44B.

runs.failure_detail: rank0 aborted in autograd backward with Available Free mem: 142 MB after the no-MTP model was confirmed. Follow-up no-helper triton_bwd run exposed the same memory cliff as forward MoE combine weighted-scatter materialization.

The triton_bwd backend keeps torch forward semantics. It still calls deterministic_weighted_scatter_add, which materializes weighted = (src.float() * scores).to(src.dtype). On rank0 this tried to allocate 3.00 GiB with only 1.21 GiB free; PyTorch had 271.76 GiB allocated and 6.64 GiB reserved.
runs.root_cause_read: Plain no-helper standard EP sends all top-k routed rows through normal combine. At MBS8/S4096/topk6 the combine source is about 196608 routed rows, so the fp32 weighted slab is about 3.0 GiB. Hot-helper avoids this memory cliff by removing hot rows from the normal EP path before combine, not merely by changing the communication primitive.

runs.failure_detail: After the no-helper triton_bwd failure, a separate nexus_titan.runtime Llama-8B fault-tolerance job occupied the node. The launcher refused to co-schedule; the process was not killed because it was not this DSv4 run.
runs.next_action: When an 8xMI350 node is idle, run this full Triton weighted-scatter no-helper probe to isolate whether removing the torch forward slab lets plain standard EP fit at MBS8.

runs.failure_detail: deterministic_weighted_scatter_add materialized weighted = (src.float() * scores).to(src.dtype) and tried to allocate 2.72 GiB with only 1.60 GiB free on rank1.

runs.next_action: Rerun this retained-style hot-helper candidate when an 8xMI350 node is idle and local git hash-object workers are drained.

runs.failure: MORI SDMA AllGather timeout waiting for peer 0
runs.failure_detail: The run reached Training starts at step 1, initialized AllreduceSdma on all 8 PEs, then PE 1-7 printed AllGather timeout waiting for peer 0. The run was terminated by run-id-specific pkill to release GPUs.

read.baseline_status: Plain standard EP without hot-helper does not fit the MBS8 attention-only-AC no-MTP surface with the current torch-forward weighted-scatter combine path.
read.nohelper_root_cause: The apparent HSA out-of-resources failure is now attributed to normal EP combine memory pressure. A follow-up run with triton_bwd produced a Python OOM at deterministic_weighted_scatter_add: triton_bwd only changes backward, while forward still materializes a full fp32 weighted routed-row slab.
read.hothelper_status: Top-8 hot-helper also needs the retained non-materializing weighted-scatter backend; the fair torch-scatter version fails in forward combine due to a large temporary slab.
read.standard_ep_hothelper_repro: Reproduced on the corrected no-MTP surface when hot-weight transport uses torch/RCCL instead of MORI SDMA: 8579.53 tok/GPU/s and 392.94 TFLOPs/GPU over steps 2-20; 8632.88 tok/GPU/s and 395.38 TFLOPs/GPU over steps 13-20.
read.mori_sdma_transport_read: The same surface with MORI SDMA hot-weight forward/reduce hangs before the first metric in the forward hot-weight AllGather path, so SDMA forward transport must be fixed or bypassed before claiming MORI improves the already-working standard-EP hot-helper baseline.
read.next_action: Use the torch-transport standard-EP hot-helper run as the fair reproduced baseline. When a node frees, run the no-helper full Triton weighted-scatter probe to separate no-helper row-volume pressure from the torch forward slab, then compare MORI-native balanced-MoE against the reproduced hot-helper baseline.
read.node_availability: After the follow-up failure, do-vunguyen-mi350-gpu became occupied by an unrelated nexus_titan.runtime Llama-8B job; it was left untouched.
